question: What is the difference between Boc and Fmoc protecting groups?
options: Boc is acid-labile, Fmoc is base-labile
Boc is base-labile, Fmoc is acid-labile
explanation: Boc (tert-butyloxycarbonyl) is removed by acid (TFA), while Fmoc (fluorenylmethyloxycarbonyl) is removed by base (piperidine). This orthogonal chemistry allows selective deprotection.
